Title: Identification and Characterization of Salt- and Drought-Responsive AQP Family Genes in Medicago sativa L.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Homologous to MtTIP1 in M. truncatula; selected as a typical salt-responsive aquaporin based on MtTIP1 expression profiles in GeneChip data.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsTIP4 is an aquaporin (MIP family) gene located on chromosome 1 of Medicago sativa.
2. MsTIP4 is strongly induced by 150 mM NaCl, with >3-fold transcript increase at 48 h compared with 0 h.
3. MsTIP4 shows preferential expression in roots and relatively high expression in stems and flowers, indicating tissue-specific roles in water transport.
4. Its salt-responsive and root-preferential expression suggest MsTIP4 as a candidate gene for improving salt tolerance and water homeostasis in alfalfa.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under 150 mM NaCl, MsTIP4 transcript decreases slightly at 4–8 h, then increases, reaching >3-fold induction at 48 h; spatially, MsTIP4 is highly expressed in roots and also relatively highly expressed in stems and flowers.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salt stress imposed by 150 mM NaCl for 0, 4, 8, 12, 24 and 48 h under hydroponic conditions.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (salt stress); aquaporin-mediated water transport.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: MsTIP4 transcript reaches more than 3-fold higher level at 48 h of 150 mM NaCl compared with 0 h.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: M. sativa (cv. Zhongmu No.1) seedlings grown hydroponically in a growth chamber at 24 °C with 16 h light/8 h dark.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qPCR analysis of MsTIP4 transcript levels under 150 mM NaCl treatment at multiple time points and in different tissues (root, stem, leaf, flower); stress treatments applied in hydroponic culture.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as part of the MsAQP family by genome-wide HMMER/Pfam and BLAST searches for AQP/MIP domains in the M. sativa genome, and selected as a representative salt-responsive MsAQP based on expression of its M. truncatula ortholog MtTIP1 in GeneChip data.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Salt-inducible aquaporin preferentially expressed in roots and also in stems and flowers; proposed as a candidate gene to improve alfalfa salt stress resistance and water homeostasis via gene editing or genetic breeding.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Homologous to MtTIP7; selected as a typical salt-responsive aquaporin based on MtTIP7 expression profiles in M. truncatula GeneChip data.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsTIP10 encodes a predicted tonoplast/mitochondrion-localized aquaporin on chromosome 4 of M. sativa.
2. MsTIP10 is induced 2–2.5-fold by 150 mM NaCl at 12–24 h.
3. MsTIP10 is preferentially expressed in roots, suggesting a role in root water transport under salt stress.
4. Its salt-inducible root expression makes MsTIP10 a candidate gene for improving salt tolerance in alfalfa.
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted localization to mitochondrion and vacuole.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under 150 mM NaCl, MsTIP10 transcript shows 2–2.5-fold induction at 12–24 h; spatially, MsTIP10 is highly expressed in roots.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 150 mM NaCl treatment for 0, 4, 8, 12, 24 and 48 h.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (salt stress); water transport and vacuolar/mitochondrial water homeostasis.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: MsTIP10 transcript increases approximately 2–2.5-fold at 12–24 h after 150 mM NaCl treatment relative to 0 h.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: M. sativa (cv. Zhongmu No.1) seedlings grown hydroponically in a controlled growth chamber.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qPCR time-course analysis of MsTIP10 transcript levels in response to 150 mM NaCl and qPCR-based tissue expression profiling in roots, stems, leaves and flowers.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from the M. sativa genome by HMMER/Pfam and BLAST searches for AQP/MIP domains; selected as a representative salt-responsive MsTIP based on the salt-responsive expression of its M. truncatula ortholog MtTIP7 in GeneChip data.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: NaCl-inducible tonoplast/mitochondrion-localized aquaporin preferentially expressed in roots; considered a candidate gene for enhancing salt stress adaptation and cellular water balance in alfalfa.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Homologous to MtTIP10; selected as a salt-responsive aquaporin based on MtTIP10 microarray expression under salt and drought treatments.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsTIP12 encodes a predicted tonoplast aquaporin located on chromosome 5 of M. sativa.
2. MsTIP12 is strongly induced by 150 mM NaCl, with >4-fold induction at 4 h and >10-fold at 12 h.
3. MsTIP12 is preferentially expressed in roots, consistent with a role in root water regulation under salt stress.
4. Its strong salt-inducible root expression supports MsTIP12 as a key candidate for improving salt tolerance in alfalfa.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under 150 mM NaCl, MsTIP12 transcript increases >4-fold at 4 h, then decreases and peaks again at 12 h with >10-fold induction; MsTIP12 is highly expressed in roots.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 150 mM NaCl treatment for up to 48 h.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (salt stress); water transport and vacuolar water homeostasis.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: MsTIP12 transcript shows >4-fold induction at 4 h and >10-fold induction at 12 h after 150 mM NaCl compared with 0 h.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Hydroponically grown M. sativa (cv. Zhongmu No.1) seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qPCR time-course analysis of MsTIP12 transcript under 150 mM NaCl and tissue-specific qPCR profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified by genome-wide HMMER/Pfam and BLAST searches for AQP/MIP domains in the M. sativa genome; chosen as a representative salt-responsive MsTIP based on expression of its M. truncatula ortholog MtTIP10 in GeneChip data.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Strongly NaCl-inducible tonoplast aquaporin highly expressed in roots; promising candidate for engineering enhanced salt tolerance and water management in alfalfa.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Homologous to MtNIP6; MsNIP2 was cloned by PCR and found to encode a 272 aa protein, suggesting that short gene models in the genome may reflect incomplete assembly.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsNIP2 encodes a nodulin 26-like intrinsic protein aquaporin located on chromosome 2 of M. sativa and is predicted to be plasma membrane localized.
2. MsNIP2 is strongly induced by 150 mM NaCl, with transcript levels increasing from 4 to 48 h and peaking at 48 h.
3. MsNIP2 is highly expressed in roots and relatively highly expressed in stems and flowers, indicating a role in root and shoot water homeostasis under salt stress.
4. Its salt-responsive and root-preferential expression profile makes MsNIP2 a key candidate for breeding salt-tolerant alfalfa.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nodulin 26-like intrinsic protein (NIP) aquaporin; major intrinsic protein (MIP) family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under 150 mM NaCl, MsNIP2 transcript increases continuously from 4 to 48 h, reaching the highest level at 48 h; tissue profiling shows high expression in roots and relatively high expression in stems and flowers.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 150 mM NaCl treatment for up to 48 h under hydroponic conditions.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (salt stress); root water transport and homeostasis.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: MsNIP2 transcript increases progressively from 4 to 48 h under 150 mM NaCl, with the highest induction at 48 h (several-fold compared with 0 h).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Hydroponically grown M. sativa (cv. Zhongmu No.1) seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qPCR time-course analysis of MsNIP2 transcript levels under 150 mM NaCl; qPCR-based tissue expression profiling; PCR cloning and sequencing of the MsNIP2 coding region for length confirmation.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from the M. sativa genome by HMMER/Pfam and BLAST searches for AQP/MIP domains; selected as a salt-responsive MsNIP based on the expression of its M. truncatula ortholog MtNIP6 in GeneChip data; MsNIP2 coding region was additionally cloned and sequenced by PCR to confirm its full length.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Salt-responsive nodulin 26-like intrinsic protein aquaporin, highly expressed in roots and predicted to be plasma membrane localized; considered a promising target for improving root water homeostasis and salt tolerance in alfalfa.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Homologous to MtNIP3; selected among MtAQP genes that showed distinct salt-responsive expression patterns.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsNIP9 encodes a NIP-type aquaporin on chromosome 5 of M. sativa and is predicted to localize to the plasma membrane.
2. MsNIP9 is strongly induced by 150 mM NaCl, with >6-fold higher transcript levels at 48 h than at 0 h.
3. MsNIP9 is highly expressed in roots and relatively highly expressed in stems and flowers.
4. Its strong NaCl inducibility and root-preferential expression make MsNIP9 a strong candidate gene for improving salt tolerance in alfalfa.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nodulin 26-like intrinsic protein (NIP) aquaporin; major intrinsic protein (MIP) family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under 150 mM NaCl, MsNIP9 transcript decreases slightly at 4 h, then increases and remains relatively stable from 8 to 48 h, reaching >6-fold induction at 48 h; spatially, MsNIP9 is highly expressed in roots and also relatively high in stems and flowers.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (salt stress); regulation of water and solute transport.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: MsNIP9 transcript shows more than 6-fold induction at 48 h of 150 mM NaCl compared with 0 h.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Hydroponically grown M. sativa (cv. Zhongmu No.1) seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qPCR analysis of MsNIP9 transcript dynamics under 150 mM NaCl and qPCR-based tissue expression profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified by genome-wide HMMER/Pfam and BLAST searches for AQP/MIP domains in the M. sativa genome; selected as a salt-responsive MsNIP based on expression of its M. truncatula ortholog MtNIP3 in GeneChip data.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: NaCl-inducible plasma membrane NIP aquaporin with high root expression; potential target for enhancing salt stress tolerance and regulation of water and small-molecule transport in alfalfa.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: MsPIP1 corresponds to the previously cloned MsPIP2;2; earlier work showed that overexpression of MsPIP2;2 in Arabidopsis increased salt tolerance, while this study shows its PEG-responsive downregulation in M. sativa leaves.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsPIP1 (MsPIP2;2) encodes a plasma membrane PIP aquaporin located on chromosome 1 of M. sativa.
2. MsPIP1 transcript is downregulated by 15% PEG treatment from 2 to 32 h.
3. MsPIP1 is preferentially expressed in leaves, indicating a role in leaf water relations.
4. Its PEG-responsive and leaf-preferential expression, together with previous overexpression data in Arabidopsis, support MsPIP1 as a candidate gene for improving drought tolerance in alfalfa.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plasma membrane intrinsic protein (PIP) aquaporin; major intrinsic protein (MIP) family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under 15% PEG, MsPIP1 transcript decreases from 2 to 32 h; in spatial profiling, MsPIP1 is preferentially expressed in leaves.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Osmotic/drought stress simulated by 15% PEG treatment for 0, 2, 8 and 32 h.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (drought/osmotic stress); leaf water transport and drought adaptation.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: MsPIP1 transcript level decreases progressively between 2 and 32 h under 15% PEG treatment compared with 0 h.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Hydroponically grown seedlings of M. sativa (cv. Zhongmu No.1).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qPCR time-course analysis of MsPIP1 transcript levels under 15% PEG treatment and qPCR-based tissue expression profiling; comparison with previous MsPIP2;2 expression data.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as a PIP-type AQP (MsPIP1, previously MsPIP2;2) by genome-wide HMMER/Pfam and BLAST searches in the M. sativa genome and by earlier cloning work; selected as a drought-responsive candidate based on expression of its M. truncatula ortholog MtPIP5 in GeneChip drought data.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Drought/PEG-responsive plasma membrane PIP aquaporin preferentially expressed in leaves; serves as a candidate gene for modulating leaf water status and drought tolerance in alfalfa, and corresponds to the previously characterized MsPIP2;2.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Homologous to MtPIP2 in M. truncatula; selected among MtAQP genes that showed drought-responsive patterns.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsPIP3 encodes a PIP-type aquaporin on chromosome 3 of M. sativa.
2. MsPIP3 transcript is downregulated at 2 and 8 h and slightly upregulated at 32 h under 15% PEG treatment.
3. MsPIP3 shows stem-preferential expression in spatial profiling.
4. Its PEG-responsive and stem-preferential expression suggests a role in stem water transport under drought and makes it a candidate gene for drought adaptation breeding.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plasma membrane intrinsic protein (PIP) aquaporin; major intrinsic protein (MIP) family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under 15% PEG, MsPIP3 transcript decreases at 2 and 8 h, then increases slightly at 32 h; tissue profiling indicates that MsPIP3 is preferentially expressed in stems.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (drought/osmotic stress); stem hydraulic conductivity and water transport.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: MsPIP3 transcript decreases at early PEG treatment (2–8 h) and shows a slight increase by 32 h compared with earlier time points.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Hydroponically grown M. sativa (cv. Zhongmu No.1) seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qPCR analysis of MsPIP3 transcript levels under 15% PEG at multiple time points and qPCR-based tissue expression profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified by HMMER/Pfam and BLAST searches for AQP/MIP domains in the M. sativa genome; selected as a drought-responsive MsPIP based on expression of its M. truncatula ortholog MtPIP2 in GeneChip data.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Drought/PEG-responsive plasma membrane PIP aquaporin preferentially expressed in stems; candidate gene for modifying stem hydraulic properties and drought adaptation in alfalfa.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Homologous to MtPIP7; selected from MtAQP genes with strong drought-responsive expression patterns.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsPIP4 encodes a PIP-type aquaporin on chromosome 4 of M. sativa.
2. MsPIP4 transcript is downregulated at 8 and 32 h under 15% PEG treatment.
3. MsPIP4 shows leaf-preferential expression.
4. Its PEG-responsive and leaf-preferential expression suggests MsPIP4 as a candidate gene for improving drought tolerance through modulation of leaf water status.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plasma membrane intrinsic protein (PIP) aquaporin; major intrinsic protein (MIP) family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under 15% PEG, MsPIP4 transcript decreases at 8 and 32 h; tissue profiling shows MsPIP4 is preferentially expressed in leaves.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (drought/osmotic stress); leaf water transport and drought resistance.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: MsPIP4 transcript is reduced at 8 and 32 h under 15% PEG compared with 0 h.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Hydroponically grown M. sativa (cv. Zhongmu No.1) seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qPCR time-course analysis of MsPIP4 transcript levels under 15% PEG and qPCR-based tissue expression profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from the M. sativa genome by HMMER/Pfam and BLAST searches; selected as a drought-responsive MsPIP based on expression of its M. truncatula ortholog MtPIP7 under drought.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Drought/PEG-responsive plasma membrane PIP aquaporin with leaf-preferential expression; candidate gene for improving leaf water-use efficiency and drought tolerance in alfalfa.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Homologous to MtNIP2; selected from drought-responsive MtAQP genes with distinct expression changes under drought and rewatering.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsNIP1 encodes a NIP-type aquaporin located on chromosome 1 of M. sativa.
2. MsNIP1 transcript is significantly induced (~2–2.5-fold) by 15% PEG at 2, 8 and 32 h.
3. MsNIP1 is most highly expressed in roots.
4. Its PEG-inducible and root-preferential expression suggests MsNIP1 as a candidate gene for improving drought tolerance through enhanced root water uptake.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nodulin 26-like intrinsic protein (NIP) aquaporin; major intrinsic protein (MIP) family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under 15% PEG, MsNIP1 transcript is significantly increased by ~2–2.5-fold at 2, 8 and 32 h; tissue profiling shows MsNIP1 has the highest expression in roots.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (drought/osmotic stress); root water uptake and homeostasis.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: MsNIP1 transcript is increased approximately 2–2.5-fold at 2, 8 and 32 h under 15% PEG compared with 0 h.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: M. sativa (cv. Zhongmu No.1) seedlings grown hydroponically.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qPCR analysis of MsNIP1 transcript levels under 15% PEG over time and qPCR-based tissue expression profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified by genome-wide HMMER/Pfam and BLAST searches for AQP/MIP domains; selected as a drought-responsive MsNIP based on expression of its M. truncatula ortholog MtNIP2 in drought GeneChip data.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Drought/PEG-inducible root-preferential NIP aquaporin; candidate gene for enhancing root water uptake and maintaining water balance under drought in alfalfa.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Homologous to MtNIP11; selected among MtAQP genes with strong drought responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. MsNIP6 encodes a NIP-type aquaporin located on chromosome 4 of M. sativa.
2. MsNIP6 transcript is induced under 15% PEG treatment, with elevated levels at 2, 8 and 32 h.
3. MsNIP6 shows highest expression in leaves in spatial profiling.
4. Its PEG-responsive and leaf-preferential expression makes MsNIP6 a candidate gene for improving drought tolerance through modulation of leaf water and solute transport.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nodulin 26-like intrinsic protein (NIP) aquaporin; major intrinsic protein (MIP) family.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Under 15% PEG, MsNIP6 transcript shows a pattern similar to MsPIP3, with changes including induction at 2 and 8 h and higher levels at 32 h; spatially, MsNIP6 shows highest expression in leaves.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response (drought/osmotic stress); leaf water and solute transport.
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: MsNIP6 transcript is induced under 15% PEG with elevated levels at 2, 8 and 32 h compared with 0 h.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Hydroponically grown M. sativa (cv. Zhongmu No.1) seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qPCR analysis of MsNIP6 transcript response to 15% PEG and qPCR-based tissue expression profiling.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from the M. sativa genome through HMMER/Pfam and BLAST searches; selected as a drought-responsive MsNIP based on expression of its M. truncatula ortholog MtNIP11 in GeneChip data.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Drought/PEG-responsive NIP aquaporin with highest expression in leaves; potential candidate for modulating leaf water and solute transport under drought in alfalfa.
